event: St Louis, St George, and the Princess
symbols: knight in armor with sword — Saint George, the dragon-slaying warrior of faith
bishop's mitre and crozier — Saint Louis's association with ecclesiastical authority and crusading kingship
princess at their feet — the maiden rescued from the dragon, symbolizing the Church or the faithful delivered from evil
vanquished dragon (implied) — the defeat of evil and Satan
description: Three figures are arranged in a vertical composition beneath an arched frame. A dark-skinned armored knight stands at center holding a long staff or lance, looking upward. To the right, a figure wears a bishop's mitre and ornate vestments. Below them, a young woman in a red and gold dress kneels or sits, her hands clasped. The background suggests an outdoor setting. The composition has a monumental, altarpiece quality.

_meta.title: St Louis, St George, and the Princess
